_meta.name: proof360 SPV Passport
_meta.description: Authored single source for proof360 — the commercial front engine and first commercial lens on the estate. Empty-but-present: empty = null | [] | explicit enum. Shared supplier rights referenced by id from CONTROL/workspace.commercial.json. Outbound architecture edges authored here; reverse (used_by) derived in the roll-up.

genesis.origin_summary: Trust readiness diagnostic: URL to scored gap report and vendor recommendations.
genesis.problem_statement: Founders and operators need a fast way to understand trust, compliance, and security readiness gaps before enterprise sale or diligence.
genesis.solution_statement: Provide a diagnostic report that turns public/input signals into readiness gaps, recommendations, and evidence-backed next actions.

contributors.name: John Coates
contributors.contribution: Concept, operating model, and product direction.

legal.notes: Standalone SPV entity not yet recorded; product is currently parent-owned.

ownership.ip_boundary: Trust readiness diagnostic workflows, scoring, report generation, and associated product packaging.
ownership.notes: Ownership structure is explicit but final intercompany documents remain pending.

architecture.role: Commercial front engine and first commercial lens. Cold-reads any entity into trust gaps, refines through CORPUS, and surfaces eligible supplier/insurer routes through FORUM.
architecture.flow_position: Front of the money flow (ESSENCE.md): proof360 cold-reads an entity -> CORPUS supplies the read + refinement -> gaps -> FORUM surfaces eligible SKUs/quotes -> attribution to PULSUS -> marketplace margin. Tetrad: CORPUS declares -> VERITAS attests -> Guard enforces -> proof360 renders.

depends_on.why: The trust read and refinement — cold-read an entity into gap signals. CORPUS is the substrate proof360 renders on top of.
Tier 2 attestation of claims before investor/customer/external use.
Each gap is routed to eligible supplier/vendor/insurer SKUs (forum_routes: ingram-micro-catalog-feed, vanta-compliance-feed), attribution preserved.

surfaces.note: Scored gap report from a public-source cold read. Guide-not-grade; shows the retrieval chain ('Retrieved from X'); no score out of 100.
Each gap -> a routed next step (vendor / distributor / AWS program / insurer / internal owner) with attribution.

external_services.name: Firecrawl (self-hosted on EC2)
external_services.purpose: Content fetch for recon (one of three recon sources).

external_services.name: Direct inference (Bedrock default)
external_services.purpose: proof360 ships inference DIRECT, not via VECTOR (ruling 2026-05-29; P9 decouple). Keys centralised in SSM.

product.target_customer: Founders, CISOs, operators, and vendors preparing for enterprise trust review.
product.positioning: Fast trust gap visibility with partner/vendor routing.
product.notes: Can consume Vanta, commercial partner, and VERITAS evidence flows.

technical.notes: HTTP product service (port 3002 on EC2) with ARGUS observability. Recon via Firecrawl+Perplexity+Gemini; inference direct.

commercial.market_category: Founder-facing trust readiness and enterprise deal readiness diagnostic.
commercial.positioning_narrative: proof360 helps founders understand which public trust gaps will block enterprise deals, fundraising, procurement, insurance, or partner conversations, then routes them toward a governed, vendor-neutral path to fix the gaps. It is a trust troubleshooting console, not a compliance audit or generic scanner.

icp.label: B2B SaaS founder approaching enterprise trust friction
icp.description: A founder-led or lean operator-led B2B company that sells into enterprise, regulated, or security-sensitive buyers and needs a fast, plain-English read on the trust gaps that could slow a deal, diligence process, partner motion, or insurance quote.
icp.company_size: Seed to Series B/growth; roughly 10-250 employees as a starting hypothesis.
icp.industry: B2B SaaS, AI, fintech, healthtech, marketplaces, cloud services, MSP/MSSP, and other companies where enterprise trust, security posture, or compliance questions affect revenue.
icp.trigger_event: Enterprise prospect sends a security questionnaire, procurement stalls, investor diligence asks for security evidence, AWS/partner program qualification begins, or cyber insurance / underwriting questions surface.
icp.pain: The founder can feel trust friction but cannot translate it into a prioritised, business-readable action plan without hiring a consultant or starting a full compliance program too early.
icp.current_alternative: Founder guesses from questionnaires, asks a vCISO or advisor, starts Vanta/Drata/Secureframe prematurely, runs technical scanners, or leaves the trust story as an unresolved sales/diligence risk.

buyer.role: Founder/operator responsible for revenue trust friction.
buyer.title: Founder, CEO, COO, CTO, VP Sales, Head of Revenue, or fractional security/compliance lead.
buyer.reports_to: Founder/board for early-stage companies; revenue or operations leadership for larger companies.
buyer.budget_authority: Likely discretionary founder/operator budget for the diagnostic; implementation budget may sit with operations, security, IT, or partner/vendor spend.
decision_criteria: Explains trust gaps in business language rather than control-matrix language.
Produces useful clarity quickly from public-source signals.
Connects gaps to enterprise deal readiness, fundraising, procurement, insurance, or partner outcomes.
Keeps vendor recommendations neutral and separated from diagnosis.
Can become a shareable or governed trust profile later without rebuilding the workflow.
objections: This looks like another compliance checklist.
The company is too early for SOC 2 or ISO 27001.
Vendor routing could bias the diagnosis.
Founder does not want another dashboard or account to maintain.

user.role: Founder or operator trying to get a trusted external conversation unstuck.
user.daily_context: Has limited time, a buyer/investor/partner asking trust questions, and needs an understandable read that turns uncertainty into a next action.
user.success_metric: User can explain the top trust blockers, knows which gap to fix first, and has a credible path to a vendor, distributor, AWS program, insurer, or internal owner.

pricing_hypothesis.model: Free or low-friction diagnostic feeding paid routing and partner economics; later per-report/subscription surfaces for enterprise buyers, investors, insurers, AWS sellers, and distributors.
pricing_hypothesis.basis: Existing proof360 commercial notes: reseller embed fees, vendor partner lead routing commission, three-branch attribution, Austbrokers underwriting model, AWS program and Marketplace motions.
pricing_hypothesis.notes: Anchor selected by John 2026-05-31: the diagnostic is free; revenue is routing/embed commission, not a founder-facing price. Keep pricing explicitly hypothetical. The first revenue test is likely founder-to-quote/vendor routing or partner/distributor embed rather than charging the founder upfront.

gtm_motion.primary_channel: Public proof360.au cold read leading to email capture and actionable Tier 2 routing.
secondary_channels: Distributor embed via Ingram/Dicker partner portal
AWS partner/program qualification and Marketplace co-sell motion
Founder-led referrals from enterprise sales and investor diligence moments
Future enterprise buyer, investor, and distributor surfaces once commercially pulled
gtm_motion.first_touchpoint: Company URL cold read or partner-distributed assessment link.
gtm_motion.conversion_trigger: A user sees a trust gap that blocks a live deal/diligence process and chooses a routed next step: John, distributor, or vendor direct.
gtm_motion.notes: Founder remains the first served persona in the locked v3 posture. Multi-stakeholder surfaces are future commercial paths and should not dilute the first ICP until John ratifies expansion.

competitive_frame.view: They help companies run compliance programs; proof360 helps founders understand which trust gaps matter before or alongside that tooling.
They speak to compliance and legal teams; proof360 speaks first to founders and revenue operators.
They surface technical findings or external scores; proof360 translates trust posture into deal-readiness consequences and supported next paths.
Strategic risk: large ecosystems may move into trust scoring. proof360's defensibility is the structured signal dataset, reasoning corpus, and portable governed profile.

messaging.headline: Know what trust gaps are blocking your next enterprise yes.
messaging.subheadline: Run a fast public-source trust read, understand the business consequence of each gap, and choose a governed path to fix what matters first.
value_props: Turns security and trust uncertainty into a founder-readable action plan.
Frames gaps by revenue, diligence, procurement, insurance, and partner impact.
Separates diagnosis from vendor routing so trust stays neutral.
Builds a structured signal dataset that can become a portable trust profile.
Routes action through proof360, distributor, or vendor direct paths with attribution.
proof_points: v3 architecture locked: deterministic recompute, override contract, VERITAS Tier 2 attestation, engagement router.

commercial.revenue_model: Free/low-friction diagnostic feeding paid routing economics: reseller embed fees, vendor/distributor routing commission, AWS program/Marketplace co-sell margin, insurance origination, future buyer/investor subscription surfaces.

attestation.basis: Best-guess placeholder drafted from proof360 DOSSIER, NORTH_STAR, strategy brief, convergence locks, and commercial notes. Not market-validated. review_needed.

finance.unit_economics_state: modelled (untested): near-zero CAC via cold-read funnel; 15-30% routing margin per closed SKU
finance.notes: Direct product costs are attributed to proof360; live revenue state remains feed-dependent.

operations.notes: Status view and ARGUS monitor operating health.

marketing.notes: Commercial register should use warmer 360 treatment once finalized.

sales.notes: HubSpot feed is registered but not treated as evidence authority.

evidence.notes: Investor/customer claims should be promoted through VERITAS before external use.

open_risks: Commercial terms and pricing remain unverified.
Standalone SPV legal formation is not recorded.
risk.notes: Unknowns remain explicit until evidence is attached.

capital.notes: Illustrative notional SPV raise selected by John 2026-05-31 to exercise the term-sheet-from-passport model. proof360 is parent-owned (ethiks360) and is NOT raising standalone — these figures are non-binding placeholders, not a live offer. Can become data-room ready after legal, finance, claims, and use-of-funds views are generated and attested.

data_room.notes: Data room is generated from this passport plus evidence registries.
